lever_summary: Generalize the dense F64 full-reduction fast path (eval_dense_f64_full_reduce, formerly _sum) from ReduceSum-only to also cover ReduceProd/ReduceMax/ReduceMin, so those reductions over dense F64 tensors fold LiteralBuffer::as_f64_slice() directly instead of materializing the 24-byte Vec<Literal> and matching as_f64() per element.

baseline.description: Same-process Vec<Literal>-backed reference bench (eval/reduce_max_64k_f64_literal_ref), forced down the generic per-element path; measured on the same worker as the final to isolate the data-model win from cross-worker CPU variance.
baseline.command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- reduce_max_64k_f64 --save-baseline maxpair

final_benchmark.description: Dense vector_f64 input (eval/reduce_max_64k_f64) hitting the generalized fast path.
final_benchmark.command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- reduce_max_64k_f64 --save-baseline maxpair

companion_reduce_sum_same_worker.note: ReduceSum dense vs Vec<Literal> measured in one process on vmi1153651 (cleaner same-worker companion to the mcqr.30.9 cross-worker pass60 number).

alien_graveyard.mapping: avoid object-at-a-time Literal materialization by folding contiguous F64 storage directly; same primitive as mcqr.30.9, now extended to the prod/max/min reductions

change.lever: eval_dense_f64_full_reduce guard widened from `primitive != ReduceSum` to `!matches!(primitive, ReduceSum | ReduceProd | ReduceMax | ReduceMin)`; single call site and fold loop unchanged. Added same-worker Vec<Literal> reference benches for sum and max.

isomorphism.ordering: unchanged: same left-to-right element order; rank-0 shortcut still precedes the fast path
isomorphism.floating_point: unchanged: the caller's float_op (ReduceProd 1.0/a*b, ReduceMax -inf/jax_max_f64, ReduceMin +inf/jax_min_f64) is applied sequentially with the same float_init seed; no reassociation, pairwise/SIMD reduction tree, or fast-math
isomorphism.errors: unchanged: as_f64_slice() is Some only for F64 dense storage; complex, integral, F32/F16/BF16, and malformed declared-F64 (Literal-backed) inputs return None and fall through to the existing generic paths
isomorphism.nan_propagation: jax_max_f64/jax_min_f64 return canonical f64::NAN (0x7ff8000000000000) on any NaN operand in both fast and generic paths

validation.name: reduction_parity_and_unit_tests
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --lib reduction

validation.name: clippy_deny_warnings
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings
